commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And Ephraim said, Yet I have become rich, I have found myself wealth: in all my labors they shall find no iniquity in me that is sin. Hebrew also, "vanity. "Riches are vain, and lead to idolatry when people place their affections on them, Matthew xiii. , and Ephesians v. I am conscious of no injustice. (Calmet) Yet he had used a deceitful balance, and his judgment is equally perverse. (Haydock) "What rich man shall be saved. "(Clement of Alexandria).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
